skill.name: deeptools
skill.description: Guides deepTools workflows for NGS data analysis, including BAM to bigWig conversion, sample QC, normalization, ChIP-seq, RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, heatmaps, and profile plots.
skill.summary: Plan, generate, and troubleshoot deepTools workflows for NGS analysis.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives from markdown command examples, deepTools option names, and genomics SAM terminology. The real concern is that the workflow generator writes bash scripts using user-supplied paths without shell escaping, so generated scripts require review before execution. No evidence found of credential theft, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious network behavior.

medium_findings.title: Generated Bash Scripts Use Unescaped User Parameters
medium_findings.description: scripts/workflow_generator.py places command-line parameter values into generated bash scripts and later uses them in shell commands. A malicious or malformed path could alter the generated script when a user executes it, although the generator itself does not run the script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script templates directly interpolate user-supplied file and directory values into bash. The risk depends on the user executing the generated script, so this is a medium-confidence issue rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command findings are markdown examples for deepTools usage, not code executed by the skill. They are expected for a command-line bioinformatics reference skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside markdown code fences or instructional examples. No evidence found that these snippets run automatically.

low_findings.title: Genomics SAM Options Misclassified as Sensitive Windows Files
low_findings.description: The SAM findings refer to Sequence Alignment/Map flags used by deepTools and BAM processing. They are unrelated to the Windows SAM credential database.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is SAM flag filtering for genomic alignment records. This is a clear domain-term false positive.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to public deepTools documentation. No code path sends user data or credentials to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear under further reading and reference sections. No network client or data transfer code was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Domain Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits map to normalization names, sequence terminology, or plotting options in documentation. No cryptographic implementation or password hashing code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain genomics and argparse documentation, not cryptographic functions. This supports a false-positive assessment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Bash Scripts Use Unescaped User Parameters
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/workflow_generator.py places command-line parameter values into generated bash scripts and later uses them in shell commands. A malicious or malformed path could alter the generated script when a user executes it, although the generator itself does not run the script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script templates directly interpolate user-supplied file and directory values into bash. The risk depends on the user executing the generated script, so this is a medium-confidence issue rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: NGS workflows require careful QC, normalization, and reproducible visualization. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code build deepTools commands, templates, and interpretation steps.
actual_capabilities: Creates bamCoverage and bamCompare commands for normalized coverage tracks.
Guides ChIP-seq QC with correlation, PCA, coverage, and fingerprint plots.
Builds computeMatrix, plotHeatmap, and plotProfile visualization workflows.
Recommends normalization methods for ChIP-seq, RNA-seq, and ATAC-seq.
Provides effective genome size values for common model organisms.
Generates reusable bash workflow templates for standard analyses.
limitations: It does not install deepTools or validate package versions automatically.
It cannot confirm biological validity without user data and experiment details.
Generated workflow scripts should be reviewed before execution.
Custom genomes require user-provided effective genome size information.

use_cases.title: ChIP-seq Quality Control
use_cases.description: Generate QC commands for replicate correlation, PCA, coverage, and enrichment checks before downstream analysis.

use_cases.title: Coverage Track Preparation
use_cases.description: Convert BAM files into normalized bigWig tracks for genome browser inspection and publication figures.

use_cases.title: ATAC-seq Visualization
use_cases.description: Apply Tn5-aware processing and create heatmaps or profiles around peak regions.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I have NGS BAM files from [experiment type]. Help me choose the right deepTools workflow and required inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Coverage Tracks
prompt_templates.prompt: Create deepTools commands to convert these BAM files to normalized bigWig tracks for [organism] using [normalization goal].

prompt_templates.title: Run ChIP-seq QC
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a ChIP-seq QC plan for input and ChIP BAM files, including correlation, PCA, coverage, and fingerprint outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete deepTools workflow for [ChIP-seq/RNA-seq/ATAC-seq] with file validation, normalization, visualization, and interpretation notes.

output_examples.input: Convert two ChIP-seq BAM files to browser-ready coverage tracks.
output: Recommends RPGC normalization with the matching effective genome size.
Includes bin size, duplicate handling, thread count, and output naming guidance.
Adds a reminder to validate BAM indices before running the commands.

output_examples.input: Check whether my ChIP-seq replicates are consistent.
output: Proposes multi-sample count summarization followed by correlation and PCA plots.
Explains that replicates should cluster together before final visualization.
Suggests fingerprint analysis to assess enrichment strength.

output_examples.input: Make a heatmap around transcription start sites.
output: Plans the two-step computeMatrix and plotHeatmap workflow.
Selects reference-point mode, upstream and downstream windows, and sorting options.
Notes that the gene annotation and coverage tracks must use the same genome assembly.

best_practices: Validate BAM, BED, and bigWig files before running any analysis.
Use the same genome assembly and normalization method across comparable samples.
Test commands on a small genomic region before full-scale processing.
anti_patterns: Do not extend RNA-seq reads across splice junctions.
Do not compare raw coverage tracks from samples with different sequencing depths.
Do not run generated shell scripts without reviewing paths and parameters.

faq.question: What data types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It focuses on deepTools workflows for BAM, bigWig, and BED files from NGS experiments.

faq.question: Can it choose the right normalization method?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains RPGC, CPM, RPKM, BPM, and readCount choices by experiment type.

faq.question: Does it run deepTools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands and scripts that users review and run in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it generate complete workflow scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. The included generator can create templates for ChIP-seq, RNA-seq, and ATAC-seq workflows.

faq.question: Does it include effective genome sizes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes common values for human, mouse, zebrafish, fly, and worm assemblies.

faq.question: Is it suitable for publication figures?
faq.answer: It can guide heatmap and profile plot generation, but users should verify visual settings and biological interpretation.
